summary: The 'Update Release Notes & API Changelogs' agentic workflow consistently fails because the Prepare phase generates 111 changed documentation files, exceeding the create_pull_request safe-output hard limit of max_patch_files: 100 configured in the workflow lock file.

bugSignals.errorMessage: create_pull_request: E003: Cannot create pull request with more than 100 files (received 111)

stepsToReproduce: The 'Update Release Notes & API Changelogs' workflow is triggered by a push to main or a release branch
The Prepare job runs the full documentation generator, touching 111 files in documentation/docfx/releases/
The agent invokes the create_pull_request safe-output to publish the changes
The safe-output fails with E003: Cannot create pull request with more than 100 files (received 111)
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: GitHub Actions workflow (update-release-notes), gh-aw v0.71.5, safe-outputs max_patch_files: 100

analysis.summary: The workflow lock file enforces max_patch_files: 100 for the create_pull_request safe-output, but the full API changelog + release-notes regeneration now produces 111 changed files across documentation/docfx/releases/ (which has grown to 1130+ total files). The fix is to add max-patch-files: 150 to the safe-outputs.create-pull-request block in .github/workflows/update-release-notes.md and recompile the lock file with 'gh aw compile'.
analysis.rationale: The error E003 explicitly states the 100-file limit and the 111-file count. Code investigation confirms max_patch_files: 100 in the auto-generated lock file, derived from gh-aw defaults not overridden in the .md descriptor. The documentation/docfx/releases/ directory has grown to 1130 files across all version subdirectories; a full regeneration touching multiple in-flight versions easily produces 111 changes. The fix path (add max-patch-files override + recompile) is unambiguous.

codeInvestigation.finding: max_patch_files: 100 is set in GH_AW_SAFE_OUTPUTS_HANDLER_CONFIG for create_pull_request. This is the hard limit that produces E003 when 111 files are generated. The lock file is auto-generated by 'gh aw compile' and must not be edited directly.

The safe-outputs section configures create-pull-request with preserve-branch-name: true and recreate-ref: true, but does NOT include a max-patch-files override. Adding max-patch-files: 150 here and recompiling with 'gh aw compile' would fix the failure.

Contains 1130 tracked files across version subdirectories (3.116.1, 2.80.2, 3.119.4, etc. with per-package API diff .md files). A full regeneration modifying multiple in-flight release pages produces 111 changed files, exceeding the 100-file PR limit.

keySignals.text: create_pull_request: E003: Cannot create pull request with more than 100 files (received 111)
keySignals.interpretation: Consistent, reproducible failure across 3 workflow runs — all hit exactly the same error with the same file count
The 100-file limit comes from gh-aw defaults not overridden in the .md descriptor

keySignals.text: Branch was updated - workflow started at 6b8e864, PR head is now 0f2861f
keySignals.interpretation: Secondary: concurrent workflow runs update the target branch mid-run; the 100-file error is the primary blocker

nextQuestions: Is 150 files sufficient headroom, or should the limit be set higher (e.g. 200) to accommodate future version growth?
Does the 'branch was updated' concurrency race also need to be addressed separately after fixing the file limit?

resolution.hypothesis: The gh-aw default max_patch_files: 100 was not overridden in the workflow descriptor, and the release-notes documentation tree has grown beyond 100 changed files per full regeneration. Adding an explicit max-patch-files override resolves the immediate failure.

proposals.title: Add max-patch-files: 150 override to workflow descriptor
proposals.description:
In `.github/workflows/update-release-notes.md`, add `max-patch-files: 150` to the `create-pull-request` safe-outputs block, then run `gh aw compile` to regenerate the lock file:

```yaml
safe-outputs:
  create-pull-request:
    title-prefix: "[docs] "
    labels: [documentation]
    draft: false
    allowed-base-branches: [main]
    preserve-branch-name: true
    recreate-ref: true
    max-patch-files: 150
```

resolution.recommendedProposal: Add max-patch-files: 150 override to workflow descriptor
resolution.recommendedReason: One-line config change in the .md descriptor directly addresses the root cause. No code changes needed; just recompile the lock file.

actionability.reason: Root cause is precisely identified (max_patch_files: 100 < 111 files generated); fix is a one-line config change in the workflow descriptor.

actions.description: Apply bug and Build area labels
Post analysis with root cause and fix instructions
actions.comment:
## Analysis

The workflow fails because the Prepare phase regenerates **111 documentation files** in `documentation/docfx/releases/`, exceeding the `create_pull_request` safe-output's hard limit of **`max_patch_files: 100`** (set in `.github/workflows/update-release-notes.lock.yml` via gh-aw defaults).

**Root cause:** The `safe-outputs.create-pull-request` block in `.github/workflows/update-release-notes.md` does not override `max-patch-files`, so the gh-aw default of 100 applies. The `documentation/docfx/releases/` directory has grown to 1130+ files; a full regeneration across multiple in-flight release versions now touches 111 files.

**Fix:** Add `max-patch-files: 150` to the `create-pull-request` safe-outputs block in `.github/workflows/update-release-notes.md`, then run `gh aw compile` to regenerate the lock file.
